title: Provancheria
intro: Provancheria, also known as Eastern American featherbells, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family, native to eastern North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Provancheria include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Provancheria include teas and infusions, as well as dried and powdered extracts used in capsule or tablet form for respiratory support.

The genus is characterized by its unique flowers with a distinct arrangement of stamens and a prominent calyx. There are several species within the Provancheria genus, including Provancheria ovata and Provancheria paniculata. These plants are often found growing in humid, shaded environments.
